title: prunus spinosa var. subcylindrica
intro: Prunus spinosa var. subcylindrica, commonly known as wild plum, is a specific variety of the Prunus spinosa species, characterized by its cylindrical shape and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This shrub is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been utilized to treat various health conditions, including wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Prunus spinosa var. subcylindrica include its ability to soothe and protect the skin, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable component in traditional herbal medicine. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried and used as a powder for various herbal preparations, such as ointments, salves, and infusions.
